ELPAC Level 2
Lexile BR–300L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

Sixty Seconds of Summary

STS-MS6-L2-BR300-MSELA6-STP-AAD-01 • MS ELA Grade 6 • Summarize Text Distinct from Personal Opinion
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
In a Book Tank pitch, you get sixty seconds to convince the judges to invest in a book, but only the story itself can do the selling. Amara and Jelani chose a novel from the Author Spotlight shelf, which celebrates storytellers from Africa and the African diaspora, and their whole pitch has to be a summary of what happens in the book. If either of them says “I loved it” or “it is the best,” that is an opinion, and the teacher hits the buzzer!
Why do you think a summary might convince a judge better than an opinion would?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Amara doing?
Look at the picture. Say: Amara is ____ at the chart. (pointing / eating)
2
What is Jelani holding?
Look at the picture. Say: Jelani is holding ____. (a book / a ball)
3
What is the teacher doing?
Look at the picture. Say: The teacher is ____ the red button. (pressing / washing)
4
What place is this? How do you know?
Look at the picture. Say: This place is a ____. (library / kitchen)
5
Tell me about the judges' table.
Look at the picture. Say: The table has ____ on it. (papers / toys)
6
What is Zuri wearing?
Look at the picture. Say: Zuri has a ____ shirt. (yellow / green)
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
Content alignment: CCSS RL.6.2 / RI.6.2 — Summarize Text Distinct from Personal Opinion. ELD (Speaking — official ELPAC “Talk About a Scene”): PI.A.1, PII.B.3, PII.B.4, PII.B.5.
